content.text:
You didn't miss it — I'd explicitly recommended deferring it ("minimal-in-Be, installer-elsewhere"), and you're now overruling that. Having sat with it: no, I don't object — I withdraw the deferral, and for a better reason than deference.

My earlier objection rested on the installer belonging to the made-side retrofit heat. But checking that reasoning: the retrofit heat owns the *Ark package family* work — the `k` colophon family, the Lode-parallel substrate. The installer is **election, not package machinery**, and the ₣BH paddock's own rule is that election lives with its consumer — yoke already exists today without any retrofit underneath it. The hallmark installer reads a fact that already exists (`rbf_fact_hallmark`, emitted by every ordain and kludge) through chaining machinery that already exists (₢BHAAQ's `BURD_PREVIOUS_DIR`) and writes a regime slot that already exists. It depends on nothing the retrofit heat will build. The dependency I was protecting was imaginary.

And there's a positive coherence argument I undervalued: Be's recast gestalt is now **in-universe testing** — tests exercise real operator verbs, not test-only channels. The graft tweak was an out-of-universe injection channel; the static-value patch merely retires it, but the installer repair *replaces it with the operator mechanism that should have existed* — the test chains conjure → summon → install → graft through verbs an operator would actually use. That's the same move as deleting the probate doors. Chaining-in-Be isn't scope creep; it's the heat's thesis applied to its third tweak.

Two cautions to carry into the docket, neither disqualifying:

- **Keep the installer's first mint narrow.** It elects a hallmark into the graft slot — don't generalize it into nameplate hallmark election, which the kludge/ordain cycles already bundle in-process by the paddock's sanctioned ergonomics. If a general election verb wants to exist, that's the retrofit heat's call. (Yoke-precedent properties carry over: lowercase, consumer-family, operator-committed, never self-committing.)
- **It's the one pace in the sequence with real minting ceremony** — a new verb word (word-selection discipline, asterism fit) and colophon against the live tree — and its test rewiring lands in the gauntlet-tier onboarding case, so its verification cadence is slower than the rest of the heat. Worth a tier hint in the Character section so the mount agent budgets for that.

So the sequence gains its shape: band paces, immure eviction, graft-chaining conversion (installer + tweak retirement + fossil-value cleanup), cloud guard, regime conversion + new fixture, enrollment/recipe/hygiene migrations, prose pace. Ready to draft the full set whenever you say go.

message.content: The very last thing I'd urge in this is a strong update of 'tweak' definition.  I have used tweaks many times in my life, and I know I've done a crap job of articulating the larger principle, but in short, one tweak at a time in any test/ fixture/ suite is enough to construct an effective set of suites that cover quite a lot of bases without massive intrusive machinery.  Are you prepared to make an appropriate update to bash and spec to define tweak as per my slightly less crappy statement above and the choices made in this chat?

content.text:
Yes — prepared, and your principle is better than you're giving it credit for. Let me restate it back in load-bearing form, because the singularity point is the part the current docs miss entirely: **the single slot isn't a limitation of the mechanism — it *is* the mechanism.** One forced condition per observation is what keeps the test seam from metastasizing into intrusive machinery, and a suite is then *composed* of single-condition observations rather than rigged with a configuration matrix. Every choice this chat made falls out of that: the collision wasn't a defect to engineer around with a second variable, it was the design telling us the poison cases belong in a different fixture.

Here's the doctrine text I'd install, capturing your statement plus the chat's choices:

> **A tweak forces one hard-to-produce condition so a test can observe the infrastructure handle it correctly** — a colliding stamp, a poisoned regime value, a denied credential path — ideally asserting the gate's named rejection code, never bare nonzero.
>
> 1. **Condition-forcing only.** An injection that enables a positive path, carries a parameter, or short-circuits work for convenience is not a tweak — it wants a CLI parameter, a regime slot, or its own colophon.
> 2. **One tweak at a time, by design.** The channel is deliberately single-slot: any test, fixture, or suite run carries at most one tweak. This is sufficiency, not poverty — one forced condition per observation is what keeps the seam non-intrusive while suites composed of such observations cover the bases. A test that appears to need two simultaneous tweaks is mis-tiered or mis-designed; a dual need that survives scrutiny mints a second mechanism deliberately — never by widening this one.
> 3. **Shape over names** (existing rule, restated): BUK validates only the `buo<segment>_` sprue; meaning is consumer-owned; `grep buo` is the registry.
> 4. **A suite may reserve the slot.** A tier may dedicate the slot to one standing guard for its whole run; cases in that tier then carry no tweaks of their own — the reservation is part of the tier's definition.

Three homes: **BUS0**'s tweak section carries the full doctrine; **`bure_regime.sh`**'s shape-enforce comment block gets the one-line form pointing at BUS0; the **CLAUDE.md BUK include**'s "BURE tweak-name detail" paragraph gets the two-line form. One honest note: the spec will briefly lead reality — the graft and immure tweaks violate rule 1 until their eviction paces land — which is fine for doctrine (it constrains births and directs the cleanup), and I'd say so in the BUS0 text with a sentence rather than pretend the census is already clean.

One placement question before I touch files: apply it now as a direct edit notched heat-affiliated to ₣Be, or carry this text as the docket body of the prose pace so it lands inside the heat's sequence? Now gets the doctrine guarding new births immediately; pace-time keeps the heat self-contained. Your call — say the word either way and I'll execute, then draft the full ₣Be reslate set.

message.content: It is an excellent idea to update BUS0 tweak section now, then to abandon current pace, then to slate the right paces to make Bb do all the steps we just discussed, then rail them into the right order.  And Fable, since you are clearly really good at complex sequences, after all that if no issues come up: review the new Heat in full and articulate concerns about errors, ambiguities, and/ or conflicts.

content.text:
Confirming one identity before I execute: you've twice typed **Bb**, but everything we've built targets **₣Be** (rbk-09-better-negatives) — the heat holding the band paces; ₣Bb is the acg-scrub heat whose rename pace *gates* Be. I'm proceeding with ₣Be; stop me if you truly meant Bb.

Sequence: BUS0 doctrine update → drop ₢BHAAl → slate the three new paces → reslate ₢BeAAC/₢BeAAF → paddock update → memo restamp → full heat review. Starting with the BUS0 tweak section.

{bure_verbose}::
Verbosity level controlling diagnostic output.
0=silent, 1=show messages, 2=enable bash trace.

[[bure_color]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{bure_color}::
Color output policy.
Input as auto/0/1; resolved to 0 or 1 during dispatch.
Respects NO_COLOR environment convention.

==== Tweak Mechanism

The tweak mechanism provides a single caller-supplied name/value override that flows through invocation without BUK interpreting its semantics.
BUK enrolls, validates, and propagates the pair; consuming kits (e.g., RBK) check the name and act on the value.

Constraint: one tweak per invocation.
The caller sets at most one
{bure_tweak_name}/{bure_tweak_value}
pair in the environment before invoking a tabtarget.
The pair is ambient — it propagates through `zbuto_invoke` like all BURE variables, without requiring re-kindle in the invoked command.

[[bure_tweak_name]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{bure_tweak_name}::
Consumer-interpreted tweak identifier.
The name selects which behavioral override the consuming kit applies.
BUK validates length (0–64 characters) and the `buo` tweak sprue — a `buo<segment>_` prefix — so an unregistered or mistyped name fails loud at enforce rather than silently no-op'ing at the consumer; BUK validates the shape only and assigns no meaning beyond it.
The sprue doubles as a virtual registry: `grep buo` enumerates every tweak across the codebase, with no central list to maintain.
+
Example: `buorb_graft_image` — tells RBK to substitute the graft source image.

[[bure_tweak_value]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{bure_tweak_value}::
Consumer-interpreted tweak payload.
The value is opaque to BUK; its format and interpretation are defined by whatever kit checks
{bure_tweak_name}.
BUK validates length (0–256 characters) but assigns no meaning to the value.
+
Either variable may be set independently — BUK does not enforce pairing.
Consumer kits that require both must enforce that themselves.

==== Exchange Labels

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURE Regime - Bash Utility Regime Environment Module
#
# BURE is an ambient regime — variables are set in the environment by callers,
# not sourced from a file. Callers export BURE_* variables before invoking.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURE_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module bure multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURE_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zbure_*)

zbure_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURE_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module bure already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.
  # Exception: ambient regime defaults for optional behavioral overrides.
  BURE_VERBOSE="${BURE_VERBOSE:-0}"
  BURE_COLOR="${BURE_COLOR:-auto}"
  BURE_COUNTDOWN="${BURE_COUNTDOWN:-}"
  BURE_TWEAK_NAME="${BURE_TWEAK_NAME:-}"
  BURE_TWEAK_VALUE="${BURE_TWEAK_VALUE:-}"
  BURE_LABEL="${BURE_LABEL:-}"

  # Enroll all BURE variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll BURE

  buv_group_enroll "Behavioral Overrides"
  buv_string_enroll  BURE_COUNTDOWN   0  4  "Countdown override (skip to disable)"
  buv_enum_enroll    BURE_VERBOSE     "Verbosity level" 0 1 2 3
  buv_enum_enroll    BURE_COLOR       "Color mode" auto 0 1

  buv_group_enroll "Tweak Mechanism"
  buv_string_enroll  BURE_TWEAK_NAME  0  64   "Tweak name (buo-sprued; consumer-interpreted)"
  buv_string_enroll  BURE_TWEAK_VALUE 0  256  "Tweak value (consumer-interpreted)"

  buv_group_enroll "Exchange Labels"
  buv_string_enroll  BURE_LABEL       0  120  "Correlation label for cross-project exchange (xname format)"

  # Guard against unexpected BURE_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURE BURE_

  # Lock all enrolled BURE_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURE

  readonly ZBURE_KINDLED=1
}

zbure_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURE_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module bure not kindled - call zbure_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURE enrollment validations
zbure_enforce() {
  zbure_sentinel

  buv_vet BURE

  # Custom enforce: BURE_COUNTDOWN must be empty or "skip"
  if test -n "${BURE_COUNTDOWN}"; then
    test "${BURE_COUNTDOWN}" = "skip" \
      || buc_die "BURE_COUNTDOWN must be 'skip' or empty, got '${BURE_COUNTDOWN}'"
  fi

  # Custom enforce: BURE_TWEAK_NAME, when set, must carry the buo tweak sprue —
  # a `buo<segment>_` prefix. BUK validates the SHAPE only (the virtual registry
  # of tweaks is `grep buo`); it never enumerates consumer names, so this stays

    esac
  fi
}

# jjezs_slate graft-chaining-installer-conversion

## Character
The one pace in this heat that mints made-side operator surface (a new election verb + colophon):
word-selection and colophon ceremony, plus test rewiring whose verification rides the next gauntlet/skirmish run.
Tier: opus-driven.

## Goal
Retire the `buorb_graft_image` tweak —
a doctrine violation per the BUS0 Tweak Mechanism section (parameter injection for a positive path;
its original dynamic-chaining rationale lapsed when the conjure-to-graft chain test retired) —
by minting the hallmark installer:
a lowercase consumer-family election verb that reads the chained hallmark fact
(`rbf_fact_hallmark`, already emitted by every ordain and kludge, via the BURD previous-dir chaining machinery)
and writes a vessel's graft-image slot.
Yoke-precedent properties: operator-committed, never self-committing, no dirty-tree self-gate.
Rewire the onboarding graft case to chain through the installer instead of tweak-injecting,
and replace the fossil graft-image value in the committed graft-demo vessel regime.

## Open at mount
Whether the rewired case chains a dynamic hallmark from a prior conjure
(tracked-regime churn per run — the yoke precedent)
or proves the installer against a staged vessel copy with a static-primed source.

## Cinched
- Installer scope is narrow: graft-slot election only.
  General hallmark election into nameplates stays bundled in the cycle tabtargets
  and any broader verb belongs to the made-side retrofit heat.
- The tweak read in the foundry director build and the theurge mirror constant both go; no shim.

## Done when
`grep -rn "buorb_graft_image" Tools/` returns zero;
the onboarding graft case passes via the installer chain on its next gauntlet or skirmish run;
fast green.

input.content:
# jjezs_slate fast-credless-guard

## Character
Hazard-closure — closes the recorded near-miss class where a PASSING fast suite spends money and mutates the depot.
Small surface: one gate at the token-mint chokepoint, one suite-runner export, one proof case, prose.
Tier: sonnet-delegable with care.

## Goal
Make fast-tier credlessness structural.
The fast suite runner sets the credless-guard tweak on every case invocation,
and the OAuth/JWT token-mint chokepoint (the rbgo/rba path) dies instantly under it
with its own band rejection code naming the violation.
This establishes the suite invariant from the BUS0 tweak doctrine's slot-reservation rule:
fast cases carry no tweaks of their own — in the fast tier the slot belongs to the guard.
Add the doctrine-pure proof case:
a deliberate cloud-verb invocation under the guard asserting the guard's specific band code.
State the convention in the theurge/test authoring context where an author of a new fast case will read it.

## Cinched
- The guard rides the single tweak slot; no new mechanism.
  The demolition condition is the doctrine's own: a genuine dual-tweak need mints the dedicated variable then.
- The gate lands at token mint, gating actual credential use — never at zipper/dispatch; zippers untouched.
- The guard's rejection code is a band code from the bubc tinder block.

## Sources
Memos/memo-20260610-heat-BH-fast-tier-credless-by-convention.md
(the near-miss record; the spot-fix seam it describes retires earlier in this heat).

## Done when
A fast case reaching token mint dies with the guard's band code by construction;
the proof case asserts that code;
fast suite green;
the convention documented in the test authoring context.

input.content:
# jjezs_reslate BeAAC

## Character
The heat's centerpiece conversion — replaces fabricated-regime probate testing with in-universe poisoning.
Opens with a short census; the conversion itself is mechanical but each case needs real reading.
Tier: opus-driven with sonnet-delegable bodies.

## Goal
Convert the regime-validation negative cases from Rust-fabricated regime files
driven through the test-only `*_probate` side-door entries
to in-universe tests:
the real validate verbs run against real regime files,
with a regime-poison tweak forcing the invalid condition —
one BUK seam in the regime-load path, post-source pre-validate,
supporting set and unset of one named variable —
and each case asserting its rejection gate's specific band code.
House the converted negatives in a NEW fixture outside the fast suite:
fast reserves the tweak slot for the credless guard, and poison cases need the slot,
so this fixture enrolls in the service, crucible, and complete suites instead;
the regime-validation positives stay in fast.
Delete each `*_probate` side-door entry as its consumers convert.
Update the CLAUDE.md test-table guidance:
regime/validation changes run fast plus the new fixture.

## Open at mount
- Baseline availability census: which regimes have a valid in-tree baseline (nameplate/vessel/repo)
  versus operator-local (station/payor/oauth/auth/depot) needing a staged valid-baseline fixture file.
- Per-gate band-code allocation against the allocation rule
  (share across alternatives, never along a pipeline).

## Cinched
- The poison seam is one membrane in BUK regime-load with set+unset semantics, carried on the tweak channel.
- The probate doors delete — no parallel survival.
- The enrollment-validation, recipe-validation, and dockerfile-hygiene fixtures are out of scope here:
  their staging shapes are already honest (inline values against the validator; the artifact under test is a file).

## Done when
The regime-validation negatives run in-universe in the new fixture asserting specific band codes;
no regime `*_probate` entry survives in Tools/;
fast green;
the new fixture green standalone via the single-fixture tabtarget.

# jjezs_reslate BeAAF

## Character
Prose craft against settled design — judgment in wording, not in shape.

## Goal
Write the BCG admission entry for the precision exit-code band.
State the design position:
differentiated exit codes are bad design, interpreting error strings is worse,
so a small per-gate band exists for exactly the tabtargets the test orchestrator asserts negatively — nothing else.
Include: band semantics (in-band means deliberate rejection, code 1 stays "imprecise death"),
the allocation rule (share across alternatives, never along a pipeline),
the enrollment requirement (no code minted outside the bubc tinder block),
and the rejected stderr-sentinel alternative with why it lost.
Cross-reference from the bubc tinder block comment.
Then sweep the tweak-doctrine touchpoints into agreement with the BUS0 Tweak Mechanism section
(landed at heat start):
the CLAUDE.md BUK include's tweak paragraph,
the fast-tier slot reservation (the credless guard),
and the in-universe poison seam as the doctrine's worked examples —
confirming the live tweak census conforms (stamp, poison, guard) with no stale exceptions.

## Done when
BCG carries the band entry; the bubc tinder block points at it;
the CLAUDE.md BUK include and BUS0 agree;
the doctrine's worked examples name the live census.

# jjezs_paddock ₣Be

## Gate

Do not start this heat until ₣Bb's bash-filename case-consolidation pace
(now first in that heat) has landed.
That pace renames PascalCase bash files across `Tools/rbk` —
including files this heat edits directly
(the rbcc constants module, the foundry-hygiene gate, the validation gate homes) —
and rename-on-one-side, edit-on-the-other is the worst git merge shape.
Once it lands, this heat runs comfortably in a parallel repo alongside the rest of ₣Bb;
verify the rename pace is wrapped via `jjx_show` on ₣Bb before mounting here.
Mind that pace's renames when reading this heat's dockets:
file references here predate the consolidation,
so resolve any stale PascalCase basename to its lowercase successor.

## Shape

Two strands, one gestalt: doctrine-pure negative testing.

Strand one — named refusals.
Theurge negative cases assert bare nonzero exit,
so a case expecting deliberate rejection also passes on any harness breakage
(unbound variable, missing file, refactor typo) —
the fast-tier fixtures carry this wrong-reason hole across their negative cases.
The fix: a small precision exit-code band, allocated per rejection gate
(roughly half a dozen codes; the census at the 2026-06-11 groom counted 6–8 gates),
homed as tinder in bubc,
projected into the theurge Rust consts by the existing zipper codegen,
and asserted specifically by the negative helpers.

Strand two — the tweak channel re-grounded (added at the 2026-06-11 groom).
The BUS0 Tweak Mechanism doctrine (landed at heat start) states what a tweak is for:
force one hard-to-produce condition for a test to observe handled correctly;
one tweak at a time per test/fixture/suite, by design;
a suite may reserve the slot for a standing guard.
This heat brings the live census into conformance:
the two non-conforming tweaks retire
(the immure convenience short-circuit becomes a real read-only dry-run colophon;
the graft parameter injection becomes the hallmark-installer election chain,
riding the existing hallmark fact + previous-dir chaining machinery),
the fast tier reserves the slot for the credless guard
(closing the recorded near-miss class: a passing fast suite that spends money and mutates the depot,
gated at the token-mint chokepoint with a band code),
and the regime-validation negatives convert from fabricated files driven through
test-only `*_probate` side doors to in-universe poisoning —
real validate verbs against real regimes, one BUK regime-load seam with set/unset semantics —
housed in a new fixture that runs in every suite above fast
(fast holds the guard; poison cases need the slot; the two never share a run).

Work order: census cleanup first (band-independent),
then band membrane + numeric codegen,
then the guard (its rejection code needs the band),
then the conversions, prose last.

## Cinched

- Exit-code band over stderr sentinel:
  a sentinel is string interpretation minimized, not avoided,
  and is swallowed by the same wrappers that launder codes.
- Per-gate codes, never per-rule —
  the hole being closed is wrong-layer failure, not wrong-rule failure.
- Allocation rule: gates may share a code only if they never co-occur
  in one test case's spawn path — share across alternatives, never along a pipeline.
- `buc_die` propagates in-band `$?` values instead of remapping to 1,
  so existing `|| buc_die` call sites need no audit or change.
- No code minted outside the bubc tinder block.
- One tweak at a time is doctrine, not limitation;
  a dedicated constraint variable mints only when a genuine dual-tweak need survives scrutiny —
  never by widening the single slot.
- Fast carries the credless guard in the tweak slot;
  fast cases carry no tweaks of their own.
  A case that needs a seam has self-identified as not belonging in fast.
- In-universe over fabrication: negatives run real verbs against real regimes wherever the
  injection point is a value; the probate side doors delete with no parallel survival.
- The guard gates token mint (actual credential use), never zipper/dispatch — zippers untouched.
- The hallmark installer is graft-slot election only;
  any general election verb belongs to the made-side retrofit heat.

## Out of scope

- tadmor-security's containment cases:
  exit codes there cross docker-exec boundaries with their own laundering rules —
  a different problem, a different heat if it ever itches.
- enrollment-validation needs no in-universe conversion:
  it already stages values inline against the validator with no file fabrication and no tweak;
  it takes only the band-code assertion migration.
- recipe-validation and dockerfile-hygiene fabrication is honest:
  the artifact under test IS a file; they take only the band-code migration.

## Done when

Every fast-tier negative case asserts a specific band code,
the survival proof lives in the BUK self-test,
the tweak census conforms to the BUS0 doctrine (stamp, poison seam, guard — nothing else),
no regime `*_probate` side door survives,
fast is credless by construction,
and the BCG admission entry documents band semantics and the allocation rule.

# Fast-tier credlessness is convention, not construction — near-miss record

Date: 2026-06-10
Status: standing-hazard record from the podvm refresh pace; the immediate instance was caught
pre-run and fixed with a buo test-seam. Triage material for the ₣BH terminal memo-walk pace.
TRIAGED 2026-06-10: slated as the fast-tier-credless-by-construction pace (₢BHAAl, pending — the poison-marker enforcement).

## The near-miss

The fast-tier podvm resolve case invokes the real `rbw-lI` tabtarget against the real working
regime — theurge isolates only BURV temp/output dirs, never credentials. On a credentialed
workstation (the operator's always is), the case would have sailed past its asserted diagnostic
into a live multi-GB Cloud Build submission, inside the `fast` suite, on every run.
Caught by inspection before any run; fixed by short-circuiting the verb under a
`buorb_immure_resolve_only` tweak.

## The standing hazard

Nothing structural makes the fast tier credless. Every existing fast fixture is safe only
because none happened to invoke a GCP-capable colophon — the first one that did nearly fired a
live build. Future fast cases can repeat this silently: the failure mode is a *passing* fast
suite that spends money and mutates the depot.

## Triage question

Whether to enforce fast-tier credlessness by construction. Candidate shapes (not designed, just
recorded): the fast suite runner exports a poison marker that the credential-load path refuses
(loud "fast tier reached a credential load" die); or theurge's fast tier asserts no network
egress; or a per-case declaration ("reaches GCP: yes/no") validated against the tier. Each has
costs; the buo-seam-per-case convention may also be judged sufficient — but then the convention
needs stating somewhere an author of a new fast case will read it.

+the guard rides the tweak slot per the BUS0 tweak doctrine's suite-reservation rule and dies at the
+token-mint chokepoint with a band rejection code (₣Be precision exit-code band).

Heat: rbk-09-better-negatives (₣Be)
Status: stabled
Created: 260610


## Paces

### evict-immure-resolve-only-tweak (₢BeAAG) [rough]

## Character
Tweak-census cleanup, first of three — small and file-local plus one fixture repoint.
Tier: sonnet-delegable.

## Goal
Retire the `buorb_immure_resolve_only` tweak —
a doctrine violation per the BUS0 Tweak Mechanism section (convenience short-circuit, not condition-forcing).
Give the immure resolve/selection path its own read-only lowercase colophon:
a dry-run/plan verb with operator value in its own right (show what immure would capture),
minted at mount-time per Word Selection against the live colophon tree.
Repoint the fast podvm-resolve case to invoke the new colophon directly,
dropping the tweak export and the seam-marker output assertion it existed to carry.

## Cinched
- The tweak read in the immure body and both theurge constants go; no compatibility shim.
- New colophon is lowercase (touches no GAR/cost state), in the Lode family.
- Full immure behavior unchanged.

## Done when
`grep -rn "buorb_immure_resolve_only" Tools/` returns zero;
fast suite green with the podvm-resolve case exercising the new colophon.

### graft-chaining-installer-conversion (₢BeAAH) [rough]

## Character
The one pace in this heat that mints made-side operator surface (a new election verb + colophon):
word-selection and colophon ceremony, plus test rewiring whose verification rides the next gauntlet/skirmish run.
Tier: opus-driven.

## Goal
Retire the `buorb_graft_image` tweak —
a doctrine violation per the BUS0 Tweak Mechanism section (parameter injection for a positive path;
its original dynamic-chaining rationale lapsed when the conjure-to-graft chain test retired) —
by minting the hallmark installer:
a lowercase consumer-family election verb that reads the chained hallmark fact
(`rbf_fact_hallmark`, already emitted by every ordain and kludge, via the BURD previous-dir chaining machinery)
and writes a vessel's graft-image slot.
Yoke-precedent properties: operator-committed, never self-committing, no dirty-tree self-gate.
Rewire the onboarding graft case to chain through the installer instead of tweak-injecting,
and replace the fossil graft-image value in the committed graft-demo vessel regime.

## Open at mount
Whether the rewired case chains a dynamic hallmark from a prior conjure
(tracked-regime churn per run — the yoke precedent)
or proves the installer against a staged vessel copy with a static-primed source.

## Cinched
- Installer scope is narrow: graft-slot election only.
  General hallmark election into nameplates stays bundled in the cycle tabtargets
  and any broader verb belongs to the made-side retrofit heat.
- The tweak read in the foundry director build and the theurge mirror constant both go; no shim.

## Done when
`grep -rn "buorb_graft_image" Tools/` returns zero;
the onboarding graft case passes via the installer chain on its next gauntlet or skirmish run;
fast green.

### band-tinder-and-die-membrane (₢BeAAA) [rough]

Establish the precision exit-code band and make `buc_die` transparent to it.

Define band tinder in `Tools/buk/bubc_constants.sh`:
band base/width plus per-gate rejection codes —
per rejection gate, never per validation rule.
Pick the band clear of shell-reserved codes (2, 126, 127, 128+n).

Teach `buc_die` (`Tools/buk/buc_command.sh`) to capture `$?` on entry
and re-exit with it when the value lies in the band;
add an origin helper for deliberate rejection sites.
Existing `cmd || buc_die` call sites stay untouched —
the membrane makes them propagate band codes for free.

Prove end-to-end survival:
a BUK self-test case where a band code raised under a `cmd || buc_die` chain
reaches the caller's captured exit status through the tabtarget/launcher exec path.

## Cinched
Exit-code band over stderr sentinel;
per-gate codes, not per-rule.

## Done when
Band tinder and membrane land;
`tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green including the new survival case.

### numeric-const-codegen (₢BeAAB) [rough]

Project the band constants into Rust as numeric consts.

Extend the rbcc emission (`Tools/rbk/rbcc_Constants.sh`, riding
`rbz_emit_consts` in `Tools/rbk/rbz_zipper.sh` → `rbtdgc_consts.rs`)
with a numeric emit shape — current emission is `&str` only,
theurge compares exit codes as integers.
bubc is sourced at emission time,
so projecting BUK-homed band values needs no cross-module tinder trick.
Write-on-change and the qualify freshness gate apply unchanged.

## Done when
Generated `rbtdgc_consts.rs` carries the band as numeric consts;
`tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` regenerates it and `tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh` passes freshness.

### fast-credless-guard (₢BeAAI) [rough]

## Character
Hazard-closure — closes the recorded near-miss class where a PASSING fast suite spends money and mutates the depot.
Small surface: one gate at the token-mint chokepoint, one suite-runner export, one proof case, prose.
Tier: sonnet-delegable with care.

## Goal
Make fast-tier credlessness structural.
The fast suite runner sets the credless-guard tweak on every case invocation,
and the OAuth/JWT token-mint chokepoint (the rbgo/rba path) dies instantly under it
with its own band rejection code naming the violation.
This establishes the suite invariant from the BUS0 tweak doctrine's slot-reservation rule:
fast cases carry no tweaks of their own — in the fast tier the slot belongs to the guard.
Add the doctrine-pure proof case:
a deliberate cloud-verb invocation under the guard asserting the guard's specific band code.
State the convention in the theurge/test authoring context where an author of a new fast case will read it.

## Cinched
- The guard rides the single tweak slot; no new mechanism.
  The demolition condition is the doctrine's own: a genuine dual-tweak need mints the dedicated variable then.
- The gate lands at token mint, gating actual credential use — never at zipper/dispatch; zippers untouched.
- The guard's rejection code is a band code from the bubc tinder block.

## Sources
Memos/memo-20260610-heat-BH-fast-tier-credless-by-convention.md
(the near-miss record; the spot-fix seam it describes retires earlier in this heat).

## Done when
A fast case reaching token mint dies with the guard's band code by construction;
the proof case asserts that code;
fast suite green;
the convention documented in the test authoring context.

### migrate-regime-validation-negatives (₢BeAAC) [rough]

## Character
The heat's centerpiece conversion — replaces fabricated-regime probate testing with in-universe poisoning.
Opens with a short census; the conversion itself is mechanical but each case needs real reading.
Tier: opus-driven with sonnet-delegable bodies.

## Goal
Convert the regime-validation negative cases from Rust-fabricated regime files
driven through the test-only `*_probate` side-door entries
to in-universe tests:
the real validate verbs run against real regime files,
with a regime-poison tweak forcing the invalid condition —
one BUK seam in the regime-load path, post-source pre-validate,
supporting set and unset of one named variable —
and each case asserting its rejection gate's specific band code.
House the converted negatives in a NEW fixture outside the fast suite:
fast reserves the tweak slot for the credless guard, and poison cases need the slot,
so this fixture enrolls in the service, crucible, and complete suites instead;
the regime-validation positives stay in fast.
Delete each `*_probate` side-door entry as its consumers convert.
Update the CLAUDE.md test-table guidance:
regime/validation changes run fast plus the new fixture.

## Open at mount
- Baseline availability census: which regimes have a valid in-tree baseline (nameplate/vessel/repo)
  versus operator-local (station/payor/oauth/auth/depot) needing a staged valid-baseline fixture file.
- Per-gate band-code allocation against the allocation rule
  (share across alternatives, never along a pipeline).

## Cinched
- The poison seam is one membrane in BUK regime-load with set+unset semantics, carried on the tweak channel.
- The probate doors delete — no parallel survival.
- The enrollment-validation, recipe-validation, and dockerfile-hygiene fixtures are out of scope here:
  their staging shapes are already honest (inline values against the validator; the artifact under test is a file).

## Done when
The regime-validation negatives run in-universe in the new fixture asserting specific band codes;
no regime `*_probate` entry survives in Tools/;
fast green;
the new fixture green standalone via the single-fixture tabtarget.

### migrate-enrollment-validation-negatives (₢BeAAD) [rough]

Close the wrong-reason hole for the enrollment-validation fixture's negative cases.

Same shape as the regime-validation migration:
teach the enrollment validation gate to reject with its band code,
flip the fixture's negative cases to assert that specific code.
The expect-code plumbing in the theurge negative helpers
lands in the prior migration pace; this pace consumes it.

## Done when
`tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh` green
with every enrollment-validation negative case asserting the gate's code.

### migrate-recipe-and-hygiene-negatives (₢BeAAE) [rough]

Close the wrong-reason hole for the recipe-validation
and dockerfile-hygiene fixtures' negative cases.

Teach the recipe validation gate and the rbfh FROM-line hygiene gate
(`Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh`) to reject with band codes.
These two gates are alternatives — never in one spawn path —
so they may share a code per the allocation rule;
confirm that at migration time rather than assuming.
Flip both fixtures' negative cases to assert the expected code.

## Done when
`tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh` green
with both fixtures' negative cases asserting specific codes.

### bcg-precision-band-admission (₢BeAAF) [rough]

## Character
Prose craft against settled design — judgment in wording, not in shape.

## Goal
Write the BCG admission entry for the precision exit-code band.
State the design position:
differentiated exit codes are bad design, interpreting error strings is worse,
so a small per-gate band exists for exactly the tabtargets the test orchestrator asserts negatively — nothing else.
Include: band semantics (in-band means deliberate rejection, code 1 stays "imprecise death"),
the allocation rule (share across alternatives, never along a pipeline),
the enrollment requirement (no code minted outside the bubc tinder block),
and the rejected stderr-sentinel alternative with why it lost.
Cross-reference from the bubc tinder block comment.
Then sweep the tweak-doctrine touchpoints into agreement with the BUS0 Tweak Mechanism section
(landed at heat start):
the CLAUDE.md BUK include's tweak paragraph,
the fast-tier slot reservation (the credless guard),
and the in-universe poison seam as the doctrine's worked examples —
confirming the live tweak census conforms (stamp, poison, guard) with no stale exceptions.

## Done when
BCG carries the band entry; the bubc tinder block points at it;
the CLAUDE.md BUK include and BUS0 agree;
the doctrine's worked examples name the live census.

File-touch bitmap: (no work file changes)

- The tweak read in the foundry director build and the theurge mirror constant both go; no shim.

## Done when
`grep -rn "buorb_graft_image" Tools/` returns zero;
the onboarding graft case passes via the installer chain on its next gauntlet or skirmish run;
fast green.

### band-tinder-and-die-membrane (₢BeAAA) [rough]

Establish the precision exit-code band and make `buc_die` transparent to it.

Define band tinder in `Tools/buk/bubc_constants.sh`:
band base/width plus per-gate rejection codes —
per rejection gate, never per validation rule.
Pick the band clear of shell-reserved codes (2, 126, 127, 128+n).

Teach `buc_die` (`Tools/buk/buc_command.sh`) to capture `$?` on entry
and re-exit with it when the value lies in the band;
add an origin helper for deliberate rejection sites.
Existing `cmd || buc_die` call sites stay untouched —
the membrane makes them propagate band codes for free.

Prove end-to-end survival:
a BUK self-test case where a band code raised under a `cmd || buc_die` chain
reaches the caller's captured exit status through the tabtarget/launcher exec path.

## Cinched
Exit-code band over stderr sentinel;
per-gate codes, not per-rule.

## Done when
Band tinder and membrane land;
`tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green including the new survival case.

### numeric-const-codegen (₢BeAAB) [rough]

Project the band constants into Rust as numeric consts.

Extend the rbcc emission (`Tools/rbk/rbcc_Constants.sh`, riding
`rbz_emit_consts` in `Tools/rbk/rbz_zipper.sh` → `rbtdgc_consts.rs`)
with a numeric emit shape — current emission is `&str` only,
theurge compares exit codes as integers.
bubc is sourced at emission time,
so projecting BUK-homed band values needs no cross-module tinder trick.
Write-on-change and the qualify freshness gate apply unchanged.

## Done when
Generated `rbtdgc_consts.rs` carries the band as numeric consts;
`tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` regenerates it and `tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh` passes freshness.

### fast-credless-guard (₢BeAAI) [rough]

## Character
Hazard-closure — closes the recorded near-miss class where a PASSING fast suite spends money and mutates the depot.
Small surface: one gate at the token-mint chokepoint, one suite-runner export, one proof case, prose.
Tier: sonnet-delegable with care.

## Goal
Make fast-tier credlessness structural.
The fast suite runner sets the credless-guard tweak on every case invocation,
and the OAuth/JWT token-mint chokepoint (the rbgo/rba path) dies instantly under it
with its own band rejection code naming the violation.
This establishes the suite invariant from the BUS0 tweak doctrine's slot-reservation rule:
fast cases carry no tweaks of their own — in the fast tier the slot belongs to the guard.
Add the doctrine-pure proof case:
a deliberate cloud-verb invocation under the guard asserting the guard's specific band code.
State the convention in the theurge/test authoring context where an author of a new fast case will read it.

## Cinched
- The guard rides the single tweak slot; no new mechanism.
  The demolition condition is the doctrine's own: a genuine dual-tweak need mints the dedicated variable then.
- The gate lands at token mint, gating actual credential use — never at zipper/dispatch; zippers untouched.
- The guard's rejection code is a band code from the bubc tinder block.

## Sources
Memos/memo-20260610-heat-BH-fast-tier-credless-by-convention.md
(the near-miss record; the spot-fix seam it describes retires earlier in this heat).

## Done when
A fast case reaching token mint dies with the guard's band code by construction;
the proof case asserts that code;
fast suite green;
the convention documented in the test authoring context.

### migrate-regime-validation-negatives (₢BeAAC) [rough]

## Character
The heat's centerpiece conversion — replaces fabricated-regime probate testing with in-universe poisoning.
Opens with a short census; the conversion itself is mechanical but each case needs real reading.
Tier: opus-driven with sonnet-delegable bodies.

## Goal
Convert the regime-validation negative cases from Rust-fabricated regime files
driven through the test-only `*_probate` side-door entries
to in-universe tests:
the real validate verbs run against real regime files,
with a regime-poison tweak forcing the invalid condition —
one BUK seam in the regime-load path, post-source pre-validate,
supporting set and unset of one named variable —
and each case asserting its rejection gate's specific band code.
House the converted negatives in a NEW fixture outside the fast suite:
fast reserves the tweak slot for the credless guard, and poison cases need the slot,
so this fixture enrolls in the service, crucible, and complete suites instead;
the regime-validation positives stay in fast.
Delete each `*_probate` side-door entry as its consumers convert.
Update the CLAUDE.md test-table guidance:
regime/validation changes run fast plus the new fixture.

## Open at mount
- Baseline availability census: which regimes have a valid in-tree baseline (nameplate/vessel/repo)
  versus operator-local (station/payor/oauth/auth/depot) needing a staged valid-baseline fixture file.
- Per-gate band-code allocation against the allocation rule
  (share across alternatives, never along a pipeline).

## Cinched
- The poison seam is one membrane in BUK regime-load with set+unset semantics, carried on the tweak channel.
- The probate doors delete — no parallel survival.
- The enrollment-validation, recipe-validation, and dockerfile-hygiene fixtures are out of scope here:
  their staging shapes are already honest (inline values against the validator; the artifact under test is a file).

## Done when
The regime-validation negatives run in-universe in the new fixture asserting specific band codes;
no regime `*_probate` entry survives in Tools/;
fast green;
the new fixture green standalone via the single-fixture tabtarget.

### migrate-enrollment-validation-negatives (₢BeAAD) [rough]

Close the wrong-reason hole for the enrollment-validation fixture's negative cases.

Same shape as the regime-validation migration:
teach the enrollment validation gate to reject with its band code,
flip the fixture's negative cases to assert that specific code.
The expect-code plumbing in the theurge negative helpers
lands in the prior migration pace; this pace consumes it.

## Done when
`tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh` green
with every enrollment-validation negative case asserting the gate's code.

### migrate-recipe-and-hygiene-negatives (₢BeAAE) [rough]

Close the wrong-reason hole for the recipe-validation
and dockerfile-hygiene fixtures' negative cases.

Teach the recipe validation gate and the rbfh FROM-line hygiene gate
(`Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh`) to reject with band codes.
These two gates are alternatives — never in one spawn path —
so they may share a code per the allocation rule;
confirm that at migration time rather than assuming.
Flip both fixtures' negative cases to assert the expected code.

## Done when
`tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh` green
with both fixtures' negative cases asserting specific codes.

### bcg-precision-band-admission (₢BeAAF) [rough]

## Character
Prose craft against settled design — judgment in wording, not in shape.

## Goal
Write the BCG admission entry for the precision exit-code band.
State the design position:
differentiated exit codes are bad design, interpreting error strings is worse,
so a small per-gate band exists for exactly the tabtargets the test orchestrator asserts negatively — nothing else.
Include: band semantics (in-band means deliberate rejection, code 1 stays "imprecise death"),
the allocation rule (share across alternatives, never along a pipeline),
the enrollment requirement (no code minted outside the bubc tinder block),
and the rejected stderr-sentinel alternative with why it lost.
Cross-reference from the bubc tinder block comment.
Then sweep the tweak-doctrine touchpoints into agreement with the BUS0 Tweak Mechanism section
(landed at heat start):
the CLAUDE.md BUK include's tweak paragraph,
the fast-tier slot reservation (the credless guard),
and the in-universe poison seam as the doctrine's worked examples —
confirming the live tweak census conforms (stamp, poison, guard) with no stale exceptions.

## Done when
BCG carries the band entry; the bubc tinder block points at it;
the CLAUDE.md BUK include and BUS0 agree;
the doctrine's worked examples name the live census.

File-touch bitmap: (no work file changes)

content.text:
Done, and thank you — that one landed warmly. Here's the full account and the review you asked for.

## What executed

1. **BUS0 doctrine landed** (committed `1f39b832`, heat-affiliated ₣Be): the Tweak Mechanism section now carries the three doctrine rules — condition-forcing only; one tweak at a time per test/fixture/suite as deliberate design; suites may reserve the slot — plus the spec example swapped from the doomed `buorb_graft_image` to the `buorb_ensconce_stamp` archetype. `bure_regime.sh`'s shape-enforce block points at it. One deliberate choice to flag: I did *not* write a "current census is nonconforming" transient note into BUS0 — spec text should be durable, and the paddock carries the conformance state instead.
2. **₢BHAAl dropped** from ₣BH, and its memo restamped to point at the successor (committed `88b480e5`, heat-affiliated ₣BH).
3. **Three paces slated** into ₣Be — ₢BeAAG (evict-immure-resolve-only-tweak), ₢BeAAH (graft-chaining-installer-conversion), ₢BeAAI (fast-credless-guard) — with placement params doing the railing, so no separate reorder was needed. Final order: **₢BeAAG → ₢BeAAH → ₢BeAAA → ₢BeAAB → ₢BeAAI → ₢BeAAC → ₢BeAAD → ₢BeAAE → ₢BeAAF**.
4. **₢BeAAC and ₢BeAAF reslated** to the in-universe conversion and the extended prose pace; **paddock rewritten** with the two-strand gestalt, the new cinches, and the expanded out-of-scope list, preserving the ₣Bb gate verbatim.

## The review — what I found

**Two defects, found and fixed:**

- **₢BeAAD carried a stale cross-reference.** Its original docket said "same shape as the regime-validation migration; the expect-code plumbing lands in the prior pace" — but my reslate of ₢BeAAC changed that pace from a fast-tier migration into a new-fixture conversion, so the plumbing promise no longer held. Reslated ₢BeAAD to own the plumbing conditionally ("check before building").
- **₢BeAAC had a scope/Done-when conflict.** Its Goal converted only *negatives*, but its Done-when demanded all probate doors die — and the **twelve positive baseline cases ride those same doors**. Reslated: positives convert to direct real-verb invocations in fast (no poison, no tweak), and the baseline-availability census now explicitly covers both.

**One genuine ambiguity, recorded rather than resolved:** guard scope when fast fixtures run inside larger suites — service/complete include the fast fixtures alongside credentialed and poison fixtures, so "the fast suite runner sets the guard on every invocation" needed disambiguating between *fast-suite runs only* and *fast-fixture cases anywhere*. The invariant holds either way, so I added it to ₢BeAAI as an Open-at-mount rather than cinching it myself.

**Three judgment-level notes that survive review, for your awareness rather than action:**

- **The ₣Bb gate got heavier.** The new paces touch `rba_Auth.sh`, `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh`, and `rbldv_Immure.sh` — all PascalCase rename targets of ₣Bb's consolidation pace. The dockets refer by module prefix rather than basename and the Gate's stale-basename warning covers the rest, but the ordering dependency is now firmer than when the Gate was written.
- **₢BeAAG's dry-run colophon inherits whatever network behavior immure's resolve path has today** (the current fast case already runs resolve fully under the seam, so this is a pre-existing condition, not a regression — but if resolve queries quay, fast carries a network-touching case either way).
- **₢BeAAH is the heat's only slow-verification pace** — its rewired test proves out at gauntlet/skirmish cadence, not locally. The docket says so honestly; just don't let a mount session claim completion on fast-green alone.

The heat is coherent: nine paces, stabled, gated on ₣Bb, paddock and dockets in agreement, and the tweak census it produces — stamp, poison, guard — is exactly your doctrine with nothing left over.

content.text:
Consolidate every bash implementation filename to one rule:
`«prefix»_«snake_name».sh`, all lowercase, prefer a single word —
retiring BCG's PascalCase carve-outs so no mixed-case `.sh` file remains under `Tools/`.

## Character
Intricate but mechanical;
the only judgment calls are the word-shortening choices on multi-word names.

## Cinched
- One rule, no carve-outs:
  plain modules, decomposition entries (`«prefix»0_`), decomposition clusters, and test-case files all go lowercase.
- Where a PascalCase name is multi-word and the prefix already carries the meaning,
  slim the word part to a single word rather than transliterating to long snake_case
  (the foundry-director-build module is the poster child).
- Doc files (`ACRONYM-Words.ext`) and tabtarget frontispieces are separate naming grammars — untouched.
- The distributed kits (buk/jjk/cmk/vvk/vok/apck) are already compliant except a single buk stray;
  the rename population is essentially rbk-internal, which is why lowercase won over uppercase as the consolidation direction.

## Done when
- A case-sensitive sweep for uppercase letters in `.sh` basenames under `Tools/` lands empty.
- A repo-wide grep for each retired basename lands clean —
  sourcing lines, zipper registry, specs, acronym maps, launcher configs.
- BCG's Naming Convention Patterns table states the single lowercase rule plainly,
  with the PascalCase rows (decomposition entry, decomposition cluster, test-case file) recut to match.
- The CLAUDE.md mint table's code-file row (`prefix_Word.ext`) and the rbk acronym map agree with the new names.
- The `fast` test suite passes; run a broader tier if touched files warrant it.

First step at mount: rebuild the rename map fresh —
enumerate offenders with a case-sensitive sweep (e.g. `find Tools -name '*.sh' | grep '[A-Z]'`)
rather than trusting any slate-time list, then confirm the map with the operator before renaming.
Case-only renames on macOS's case-insensitive filesystem want `git mv`.
Single-operator caution: land renames and reference updates in the same commit so the tree never sources a missing file.

content: I wouldn't object at all if we pick new filenames now.  Would that help?

content.text:
Amended — ₢BbAAL now carries the records-exemption. Two things about how I shaped it:

**What the amendment says:** JJK-managed artifacts (paddocks, dockets, gallops state) and `Memos/` are *record surfaces* — they reference basenames current as of their writing, by design. The rename pace must not chase renames into them, and its retired-basename grep disregards hits there; the Done-when now distinguishes "living surfaces" (sourcing lines, zipper, specs, acronym maps, launchers — must land clean) from exempt records.

**What I deliberately didn't do:** name ₣Be or its paces in the amendment. A list of "heats whose dockets reference these files" is exactly the site-enumeration cruft the docket discipline warns about — it would be stale the moment another heat slates a pace touching `rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh`. The exemption is generic and durable: *any* heat gated on this pace carries its own resolve-stale guidance (₣Be's Gate paragraph already does, verbatim: "resolve any stale PascalCase basename to its lowercase successor"). The two sides now interlock without either naming the other — Bb's pace knows records self-heal; Be's Gate knows how to heal.

So the dependency is now protected from both directions: a ₢BbAAL mount agent won't mangle or fail on docket text, and a ₣Be mount agent won't trust a dead basename.

message.content: I wouldn't object at all if we pick new filenames now.  Would that help?

content.text:
Yes, it would help — concretely: the docket's only judgment content is the word-slimming choices, and those are operator calls. Cinching them now makes ₢BbAAL fully delegable (no mid-pace operator session for map confirmation), and everything we author between now and then can stop investing in dying names. The fresh-sweep instruction stays for *population* discovery (new strays); what we'd record now are *decisions*, which don't go stale the way site lists do — an entry for a file that later vanishes is harmlessly inert.

Let me get the real offender population rather than working from the acronym map.

content.text:
The sweep found **56 offenders** — and it immediately vindicated the fresh-sweep rule: the docket's "single buk stray" claim was wrong (there are 7: `bupr_PresentationRegime.sh` plus six `buts/butc*` test-case files). Here's the proposed map, grouped by how much judgment each needs.

## Group 1 — Case-only, no judgment (28 files)

Word part is already a single word; just lowercase it. `rba_auth`, `rbcc_constants`, `rbgc_constants`, `rbfcp_plumb`, `rbfld_delete`, `rbfln_inventory`, `rbflw_wrest`, `rbfly_yoke`, `rbgb_buckets`, `rbge_rest`, `rbgg_governor`, `rbgi_iam`, `rbgo_oauth`, `rbgp_payor`, `rbq_qualify`, `rbuh_http`, `rbupmis_scrub`, `rbld0_lode`, `rbldb_bole`, `rbldd_delete`, `rbldl_lifecycle`, `rbldr_reliquary`, `rblds_spine`, `rbldv_immure`, `rbldw_underpin`, `rbho0_onboarding`, `rbhp0_payor`, `rbhw0_windows`.

## Group 2 — Multi-word, slim to one (the judgment calls)

The prefix already carries the compound meaning; the word part keeps only the distinguishing noun:

| Current | Proposed | Note |
|---------|----------|------|
| `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild` | `rbfd_director` | the poster child; "build" is vaguer and trodden |
| `rbdc_DerivedConstants` | `rbdc_derived` | avoids a third `*_constants` |
| `rbgd_DepotConstants` | `rbgd_depot` | same motive |
| `rbfc0_FoundryCore` | `rbfc0_core` | |
| `rbfca_StepAssembly` | `rbfca_assembly` | |
| `rbfcb_BuildHost` | `rbfcb_host` | |
| `rbfcg_GarRest` | `rbfcg_gar` | `rbge_rest` already owns the bare "rest" word-slot |
| `rbfcv_VesselResolution` | `rbfcv_resolve` | alt: `_vessel` |
| `rbfh_FoundryHygiene` | `rbfh_hygiene` | |
| `rbfl0_FoundryLedger` | `rbfl0_ledger` | |
| `rbfr_FoundryRetriever` | `rbfr_retriever` | |
| `rbfv_FoundryVerify` | `rbfv_verify` | |
| `rbga_ArtifactRegistry` | `rbga_registry` | |
| `rbgl_GarLayout` | `rbgl_layout` | |
| `rbgv_AccessProbe` | `rbgv_probe` | |

## Group 3 — BUK strays

`bupr_PresentationRegime` → `bupr_regime` (matches the `burc_regime`/`burs_regime`/`bure_regime` sibling pattern). The six test-case files, where the `butc«xx»_` code letters already carry the case identity: `butcbe_bure`, `butcbx_burx`, `butcfc_facts`, `butckk_kick`, `butclc_links`, `butcym_yelp`. These are the loosest proposals — happy to take your words instead.

## Group 4 — One genuine fork: ABANDONED + FUTURE (6 files)

`vov_veiled/ABANDONED-github/` (5 files, including two both named `GithubContainerRegistry` under different prefixes) and `FUTURE/rbv_PodmanVM.sh`. The "no mixed-case under Tools/" Done-when forces renaming dead code; the records-exemption logic we just added to the docket argues these are records like Memos. My lean: **exempt both dirs as record surfaces** (extend the existing Cinched exemption to name them), keeping the sweep gate as "no mixed-case `.sh` outside record surfaces." Renaming them is also fine — cheap and mechanical — if you prefer the cleaner absolute rule.

---

If you ratify (with whatever adjustments), I'll fold the map into ₢BbAAL as a Cinched name map — decisions, not site discovery, so the fresh-sweep instruction stays for population and the mount becomes fully delegable. Which way on Group 4, and any word vetoes in Groups 2–3?

== rbha_GithubActions
  # Check required environment
  test -n "${RBRG_PAT:-}"      || buc_die "RBRG_PAT not set"
  test -n "${BURD_TEMP_DIR:-}"  || buc_die "BURD_TEMP_DIR not set"
  test -n "${BURD_NOW_STAMP:-}" || buc_die "BURD_NOW_STAMP not set"

  # Module Variables (zrbha_*)
== rbhcr_GithubContainerRegistry
  # Check required environment
  test -n "${RBRR_REGISTRY_OWNER:-}" || buc_die "RBRR_REGISTRY_OWNER not set"
  test -n "${RBRR_REGISTRY_NAME:-}"  || buc_die "RBRR_REGISTRY_NAME not set"
  test -n "${RBG_RUNTIME:-}"         || buc_die "RBG_RUNTIME not set"
  test -n "${BURD_TEMP_DIR:-}"        || buc_die "BURD_TEMP_DIR not set"

== rbhh_GithubHost
  # Check required environment
  test -n "${RBRR_REGISTRY_OWNER:-}" || buc_die "RBRR_REGISTRY_OWNER not set"
  test -n "${RBRR_REGISTRY_NAME:-}"  || buc_die "RBRR_REGISTRY_NAME not set"
  test -n "${RBRR_HISTORY_DIR:-}"    || buc_die "RBRR_HISTORY_DIR not set"
  test -n "${BURD_TEMP_DIR:-}"        || buc_die "BURD_TEMP_DIR not set"

== rbhim_GithubContainerRegistry
  # Module variables
  readonly ZRBHIM_KINDLED=1
}

zrbhim_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBHIM_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbhim not kindled - call zrbhim_kindle first"
== rbhr_GithubRemote
  # Check required environment
  test -n "${BURD_TEMP_DIR:-}"             || buc_die "BURD_TEMP_DIR not set"
  test -n "${BURD_NOW_STAMP:-}"            || buc_die "BURD_NOW_STAMP not set"
  test -n "${RBRR_BUILD_ARCHITECTURES:-}" || buc_die "RBRR_BUILD_ARCHITECTURES not set"
  test -n "${RBRR_HISTORY_DIR:-}"         || buc_die "RBRR_HISTORY_DIR not set"
  test -n "${RBRR_REGISTRY_OWNER:-}"      || buc_die "RBRR_REGISTRY_OWNER not set"
== rbv_PodmanVM
#
# Recipe Bottle VM - Podman Virtual Machine Management

######################################################################
# WARNING: This implementation targets GHCR (GitHub Container Registry).
# It has NOT been ported to Google Artifact Registry (GAR/Depot).
# The operations are functionally correct but the registry target is
# obsolete for this project.

content.content:
120	
121	== Implementation Status
122	
123	The current implementation exists in Tools/rbk/rbv_PodmanVM.sh and targets GitHub Container Registry. This bash module implements the mirror, fetch, init, start, stop, and nuke operations as described above. However, the check operation, which would discover upstream updates by comparing digests, has no bash implementation and exists only as a concept.
124	
125	The codebase reflects an evolution well beyond the original specifications. Earlier specification documents RBSVC and RBSVM described a simpler design in which crane would copy entire images directly from upstream to the registry. This design envisioned variables like rbrr_chosen_vmimage_fqin and rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha that would reference complete images. These variables were never implemented in code and remain spec-only ghosts.
126	
127	The actual bash implementation evolved to a more sophisticated approach involving blob-level extraction, repackaging of disk images as container images, support for both WSL and standard image families, iteration across multiple platform variants, and identity stamping for build traceability. This evolution reflects lessons learned during implementation about the structure of OCI VM artifacts and the need for fine-grained control over individual disk images.
128	
129	The vme.extractor.sh script was an earlier standalone tool for manifest introspection. It has been superseded by the zrbv_process_image_type function in the modern bash module, which integrates manifest parsing directly into the mirroring workflow.
130	
131	The file rbp.podman.mk contains historical Makefile prototypes from the GHCR era. These Makefile targets served as an initial exploration of the VM mirroring workflow but have been fully superseded by the rbv_PodmanVM.sh module, which provides a more robust and maintainable implementation.
132	
133	The implementation has never been ported from GitHub Container Registry to Google Artifact Registry. This migration remains necessary for production use within the Recipe Bottle architecture, which standardizes on Google Cloud infrastructure for its depot and service account management.

file.content:
== Implementation Status

The current implementation exists in Tools/rbk/rbv_PodmanVM.sh and targets GitHub Container Registry. This bash module implements the mirror, fetch, init, start, stop, and nuke operations as described above. However, the check operation, which would discover upstream updates by comparing digests, has no bash implementation and exists only as a concept.

The codebase reflects an evolution well beyond the original specifications. Earlier specification documents RBSVC and RBSVM described a simpler design in which crane would copy entire images directly from upstream to the registry. This design envisioned variables like rbrr_chosen_vmimage_fqin and rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha that would reference complete images. These variables were never implemented in code and remain spec-only ghosts.

The actual bash implementation evolved to a more sophisticated approach involving blob-level extraction, repackaging of disk images as container images, support for both WSL and standard image families, iteration across multiple platform variants, and identity stamping for build traceability. This evolution reflects lessons learned during implementation about the structure of OCI VM artifacts and the need for fine-grained control over individual disk images.

The vme.extractor.sh script was an earlier standalone tool for manifest introspection. It has been superseded by the zrbv_process_image_type function in the modern bash module, which integrates manifest parsing directly into the mirroring workflow.

The file rbp.podman.mk contains historical Makefile prototypes from the GHCR era. These Makefile targets served as an initial exploration of the VM mirroring workflow but have been fully superseded by the rbv_PodmanVM.sh module, which provides a more robust and maintainable implementation.

The implementation has never been ported from GitHub Container Registry to Google Artifact Registry. This migration remains necessary for production use within the Recipe Bottle architecture, which standardizes on Google Cloud infrastructure for its depot and service account management.

#!/bin/bash
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle VM - Command Line Interface

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"

# CLI-specific environment function
zrbv_furnish() {
  # Handle documentation mode
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "RBV_TEMP_DIR          " "Empty temporary directory"
  buc_doc_env "RBV_RBRR_FILE         " "File containing the RBRR constants"
  buc_doc_env "RBV_RBRS_FILE         " "File containing the RBRS constants"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/.."
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrm_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrs_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbv_PodmanVM.sh"

  # Initialize enrollment and validate environment
  zbuv_kindle
  buv_dir_exists  "${RBV_TEMP_DIR}"
  buv_dir_empty   "${RBV_TEMP_DIR}"
  buv_file_exists "${RBV_RBRR_FILE}"
  buv_file_exists "${RBV_RBRS_FILE}"

  # Source config files (CLI handles all sourcing)
  source              "${RBV_RBRR_FILE}"   || buc_die "Failed to source RBRR config"
  source              "${RBCC_rbrd_file}"  || buc_die "Failed to source RBRD config"
  source              "${RBCC_rbrm_file}"  || buc_die "Failed to source RBRM config"
  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrd_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbrd_enforce
  zrbrm_kindle
  zrbrm_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle

  source              "${RBV_RBRS_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRS config"
  zrbrs_kindle
  zrbrs_enforce

  # Start implementation module
  zrbv_kindle
}

# Execute command
buc_execute rbv_ "Recipe Bottle VM - Podman Virtual Machine Management" zrbv_furnish "$@"

# eof

# jjezs_reslate BbAAL

Consolidate every bash implementation filename to one rule:
`«prefix»_«snake_name».sh`, all lowercase, prefer a single word —
retiring BCG's PascalCase carve-outs so no mixed-case `.sh` file remains under `Tools/`.

## Character
Mechanical throughout — the word-shortening judgment calls are already settled (see the ratified name map below).

## Cinched
- One rule, no carve-outs:
  plain modules, decomposition entries (`«prefix»0_`), decomposition clusters, and test-case files all go lowercase.
- Doc files (`ACRONYM-Words.ext`) and tabtarget frontispieces are separate naming grammars — untouched.
- Name map ratified with the operator 2026-06-11.
  Single-word PascalCase basenames simply lowercase (e.g. `rba_Auth` → `rba_auth`).
  The multi-word slimmings are settled — these are decisions, not site discovery,
  and any new offender the mount sweep finds follows the same slim-to-one-word rule:
  `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild`→`rbfd_director`, `rbdc_DerivedConstants`→`rbdc_derived`,
  `rbgd_DepotConstants`→`rbgd_depot`, `rbfc0_FoundryCore`→`rbfc0_core`,
  `rbfca_StepAssembly`→`rbfca_assembly`, `rbfcb_BuildHost`→`rbfcb_host`,
  `rbfcg_GarRest`→`rbfcg_gar`, `rbfcv_VesselResolution`→`rbfcv_resolve`,
  `rbfh_FoundryHygiene`→`rbfh_hygiene`, `rbfl0_FoundryLedger`→`rbfl0_ledger`,
  `rbfr_FoundryRetriever`→`rbfr_retriever`, `rbfv_FoundryVerify`→`rbfv_verify`,
  `rbga_ArtifactRegistry`→`rbga_registry`, `rbgl_GarLayout`→`rbgl_layout`,
  `rbgv_AccessProbe`→`rbgv_probe`,
  `bupr_PresentationRegime`→`bupr_regime` (matches the regime-file sibling pattern),
  and the buts test cases `butcbe_bure`, `butcbx_burx`, `butcfc_facts`,
  `butckk_kick`, `butclc_links`, `butcym_yelp`.
- The `ABANDONED-github/` and `FUTURE/` files are already renamed
  (zero-consumer slice landed ahead of this pace) — out of the population.
- Records are exempt from the rename sweep and from the retired-basename grep:
  JJK-managed artifacts (paddocks, dockets, gallops state) and `Memos/` reference
  current basenames as of their writing, by design.
  Other heats are gated on this pace and carry their own resolve-stale-basename guidance —
  do not chase renames into JJK artifacts or memos, and do not count their hits as sweep failures.
  Git history likewise stays untouched.

## Done when
- A case-sensitive sweep for uppercase letters in `.sh` basenames under `Tools/` lands empty.
- A repo-wide grep for each retired basename lands clean in living surfaces —
  sourcing lines, zipper registry, specs, acronym maps, launcher configs —
  with hits in the exempt record surfaces (JJK artifacts, `Memos/`) disregarded per the Cinched exemption.
- BCG's Naming Convention Patterns table states the single lowercase rule plainly,
  with the PascalCase rows (decomposition entry, decomposition cluster, test-case file) recut to match.
- The CLAUDE.md mint table's code-file row (`prefix_Word.ext`) and the rbk acronym map agree with the new names.
- The `fast` test suite passes; run a broader tier if touched files warrant it.

First step at mount: rebuild the population fresh —
enumerate offenders with a case-sensitive sweep (e.g. `find Tools -name '*.sh' | grep '[A-Z]'`)
rather than trusting any slate-time list;
the names themselves are cinched above, so no operator confirmation round is needed
unless the sweep surfaces an offender absent from the map whose slimming is ambiguous.
Case-only renames on macOS's case-insensitive filesystem want `git mv`.
Single-operator caution: land renames and reference updates in the same commit so the tree never sources a missing file.

content.text:
₢BbAAL is fully cinched. Summary of this slice:

**Renames landed** (commit `40cfd4d5`, ₣Bb heat-affiliated): all six dead files lowercase — `rbha_actions`, `rbhcr_registry`, `rbhh_host`, `rbhim_images`, `rbhr_remote`, `rbv_podvm` — plus the two reference repoints the verify caught despite "nothing refers to them": the parked `rbv_cli.sh` source line, and RBSPV's two prose mentions (whose path was already stale and now reads true: `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/FUTURE/rbv_podvm.sh`). Tree is clean.

**₢BbAAL reslated**: the ratified name map is now Cinched (decisions, not site lists — a vanished file's entry is inert, a new offender follows the stated rule), ABANDONED/FUTURE marked out-of-population, Character upgraded from "intricate but mechanical" to "mechanical throughout," and the mount-time operator-confirmation round removed — the pace is now fully delegable. I also silently dropped the docket's "single buk stray" claim, since the sweep proved it wrong (seven strays), which is worth noting as the fresh-sweep rule paying for itself before the pace even mounted.

**Remaining questions, as promised — only two, both small:**

1. **The six dead-file words were mine, not ratified.** Group 4's names weren't in the table you approved — I picked `actions`, `registry`, `host`, `images`, `remote`, `podvm` from the module headers during execution. They're abandoned code so the stakes are near zero, but say the word if any rankles and I'll re-mv (`rbhim_images` was the one real guess — its header doesn't state what "im" abbreviates).
2. **₣Be racing status**: it's still `stabled`, which matches its Gate (blocked on ₢BbAAL landing). If you want it visible in your racing muster as next-up work, I can flip it racing despite the gate — otherwise leaving it stabled until the rename lands is the cleaner signal. My lean is leave it.

Beyond those, the day's structure is complete: doctrine in BUS0, ₢BHAAl dropped and restamped, ₣Be rebuilt at nine paces in dependency order, and ₢BbAAL sharpened from both directions.

content.text:
₣Be is racing. Now the two judgments:

## Will separate-repos-then-merge work?

**Yes, with one ordering condition and three known merge surfaces.** The condition: the big rename must land *before* the repos fork — rename-on-one-branch vs edit-on-the-other is the worst merge shape git offers, and ₣Be edits at least six of the rename's targets (`rba_Auth`, `rbgo_OAuth`, `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild`, `rbldv_Immure`, `rbfh_FoundryHygiene`, `rbcc_Constants`). That's exactly what the Gate guards, and your restring idea makes it structural rather than disciplinary — more below.

Post-fork, the residual conflict surface between the two repos is content-grade, mergeable but worth knowing:

- **`rbfd_director.sh` is the hot file**: ₣Bb's pilot-audit-conjure pace edits it (allocation fixes) and ₢BeAAH edits it (graft tweak removal). Sequencing one of those late, or just accepting a content merge there, both work.
- **₣Bb's broad sweeps** (the noun-cluster paces, the rbrv-env sweeps) have wide rbk blast radius that can brush ₣Be's regime and foundry edits.
- **Shared single files**: CLAUDE.md (₢BeAAC's test-table line, ₢BeAAF's BUK-include sync vs any ₣Bb registry additions) and BCG (₢BeAAF's band entry vs ₣Bb's table recuts — though the BCG naming-table recut rides the rename pace, so it's pre-fork if you restring).

One mechanical caution that isn't about code: **both repos will mutate the gallops state** on every jjx operation, so the merge will hit `jjg_gallops.json` conflicts. You've clearly run multi-officium before, so this may be solved practice — but two *clones* diverging for days is a bigger gallops delta than two sessions in one tree. Worth deciding the merge ritual for that file before forking, not during the merge.

## Restring the rename to end of ₣BH — I agree, with a placement note

Your instinct converts a behavioral rule ("don't start Be until ₢BbAAL lands") into heat order, which is the JJK-native way to express dependency: ₣BH retires first → the rename rides its tail → both successor heats fork from a post-rename commit, and ₣Bb *also* benefits (its pilot audits touch rename targets too). The pace is fully delegable now (names cinched, no operator round), so it won't slow ₣BH's retirement meaningfully.

Placement within ₣BH: **after the vocab scrub, before the terminal triage pace.** The scrub's KILL stems are content-words, not basenames, so order barely matters — but keeping triage terminal preserves its closing-duties role, and the scrub's "runs LAST after every cutover" claim stays honest since a rename isn't a cutover.

Two follow-throughs the transfer requires, which I'll do with it: **rewrite ₣Be's Gate paragraph** (it names "₣Bb's bash-filename case-consolidation pace" — that pointer goes stale on transfer; same staleness class we've been hunting all day), and check ₣Bb's paddock for any first-pace reference needing the same prune. Gestalt-purity is the one counterargument — a filename consolidation is foreign to Lode-capture — but ₣BH's tail is already housekeeping (scrub, memo shelving), so it fits the company it keeps.

Say go and I'll transfer ₢BbAAL into ₣BH, position it after the scrub, and repoint the ₣Be Gate.
Transferred as ₢BHAAp. Positioning it after the scrub, before terminal triage.

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣Bb
2	
3	## Shape
4	
5	Pre-MVP source/spec hygiene grown into the ACG audit program, in two strata.
6	First, targeted allocation sweeps feathered in pace-at-a-time alongside MVP
7	feature finalization — magic-string -> constant sweeps and noun-cluster
8	curation, each with a blast radius that merges trivially.
9	Second, the audit program: per-pair spec/module audits under the ACG move
10	catalog, run while ACG is young so the guide and the documents harden together.
11	A seed, not a tree: scope stays tight to the source/spec allocation family.
12	Resist becoming the junk drawer for unrelated cleanups.
13	
14	## Governing idea
15	
16	The authority is ACG (Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/ACG-AllocationCodingGuide.md) —
17	the spine, the three homes, the move discipline, and the numbered move catalog
18	all live there.
19	This paddock cites and never restates
20	(ACG's statements -> definition-sites clause applied to itself;
21	an earlier revision of this paddock paraphrased the guide and has been drained).
22	
23	## Cinched
24	
25	- ACG = "Allocation Coding Guide" (not "Asciidoc" — the guide governs source
26	  authoring/allocation; asciidoc is MCM's domain).
27	  Veiled/proprietary, guide-family sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG.
28	  Not released at MVP.
29	- v1 posture: bless and name emergent practice; no mandated universals;
30	  no qualify-time enforcement.
31	  Universals are candidates confirmed pace-by-pace.
